meaning: adj.趋于时尚的,流行的,风行的
examples: Deposits may be back in fashion, but they are not for the faddish.
The paintings of Li Geye never belong to the category of faddish esthetics.
"Collectibles" are faddish. They come and go. When everyone knows it's a "collectible," the game is over.
Mr. Marchionne held up hydrogen fuel-cell propulsion as an example of faddish technology that proved impractical.
Red Bull is the drink of choice for this hip, nervous, faddish bunch of characters.
